tweets.text:
After a 2008 deportation, violent Salvadoran illegal alien Arsenio Valladares has now earned the chance to stay in the U.S. for up to 10 years – in prison – for sneaking back in.

His prior convictions include: 
• Assault and battery with a deadly weapon
• Assault and battery on a police officer 
• Driving under the influence
• Larceny
• Malicious destruction of property

Perhaps he missed the food?

He’s subject to deportation after serving his sentence.

tweets.text:
Daniel P. Meenehan, 64, was charged with attempting to produce child sex abuse material following an ICE investigation.

Meenehan was employed by the TSA when ICE special agents in Missouri got a tip from the Miller County Sheriff’s office.

Our investigation and the complaint allege he made over 40 payments to child victims in exchange for nude images.

If convicted, Meenehan faces a prison sentence of up to 30 years without parole.

tweets.text:
Former missionary William James Purdy of West Valley, Utah, faces charges related child exploitation outside the United States following an investigation by ICE Homeland Security Investigations in partnership with the @StateDeptDSS. 

Court documents indicate that Purdy, a U.S. citizen, traveled to Tonga as a missionary with The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints and sexually abused and exploited over a dozen young boys. 

Foreign authorities tried to hold him accountable, but he fled back to the United States — where he found NO REFUGE and was charged July 16.   

HSI’s global reach and partnerships are crucial assets in our relentless fight against child predators. 

💻Search “HSI" on our website to see how ICE protects the nation from a lot more than just illegal aliens.
